title: Haplopappus glutinosus
taxonomy: Haplopappus glutinosus is a flowering plant species within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae and is a part of the tribe Astereae. The genus Haplopappus is further classified into the subtribe Bellidinae. Haplopappus glutinosus is native to South America, specifically in the Andean region. This plant classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and distribution within the plant kingdom.
uses: Haplopappus glutinosus has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America. The plant's properties have been studied for their potential antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's extracts have also been investigated for their antioxidant and wound-healing properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Haplopappus glutinosus as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Haplopappus glutinosus, also known as the sticky hop clover or sticky hop, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems contain flavonoids, triterpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and gastrointestinal problems. Its antiseptic properties are particularly useful in treating minor cuts and scrapes, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection. constituents: Haplopappus glutinosus, also known as sticky woolly aster, is a plant species with reported medicinal properties.  Its active constituents include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Research has also identified flavonoids like apigenin and kaempferol, which may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. The exact composition and concentration of active constituents in Haplopappus glutinosus may vary depending on factors like geographic location and plant part.

intro: Haplopappus glutinosus, also known as Resin Bush or Sticky Everlasting, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been linked to various health benefits, including wound healing, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Haplopappus glutinosus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and gastrointestinal complaints.
